---
title: "Branched Hedge-Hyssop (Gratiola ramosa)"
slug: "gratiola-ramosa"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/622613895/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Branched Hedge-Hyssop"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Lamiales"
tax_family: "Plantaginaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Lamiales", "Plantaginaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/622613895/medium.jpg" alt="Branched Hedge-Hyssop" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/stevecollins">
stevecollins</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341953302">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Lamiales
-   **Family:** Plantaginaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=163369)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats.

## Natural history notes

A small wetland plant characterized by its branching stems, narrow leaves, and tiny white to pale lavender flowers. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and wet flatwoods. It grows during the warmer months and is often overlooked due to its small size, occurring as a subtle component of wetland vegetation.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/163369-Gratiola-ramosa)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3317)
